title: Breach Log / Incident Register ‚Äî Immutable Chronological Record

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security ¬ß164.402 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security ¬ß164.402 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.10.48: Breach Log / Incident Register ‚Äî Immutable Chronological Record.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.48' running on index=privacy sourcetype=hipaa:breach_register (register_id, incident_title, status, severity, owner), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_register" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_update earliest(_time) as opened latest(incident_title) as incident_title values(owner) as owner by register_id
| eval days_open=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where last_status IN ("Draft","Investigating") AND days_open>30
| table register_id, incident_title, last_status, owner, days_open, last_update
| sort - days_open
description: Maintains a searchable chronological register of all breach-suspected and confirmed incidents with status transitions for OCR-style examinations.
value: Maintains a searchable chronological register of all breach-suspected and confirmed incidents with status transitions for OCR-style examinations.
implementation: (1) Append-only sourcetype from GRC; (2) restrict to privacy/legal roles; (3) export annually to offline counsel archive.
visualization: Timeline (status transitions), Table (stale investigations), Single value (open incidents >30d).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Maintains a searchable chronological register of all breach-suspected and confirmed incidents with status transitions for OCR-style examinations ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_register` (`register_id`, `incident_title`, `status`, `severity`, `owner`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Append-only sourcetype from GRC; (2) restrict to privacy/legal roles; (3) export annually to offline counsel archive.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_register" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_update earliest(_time) as opened latest(incident_title) as incident_title values(owner) as owner by register_id
| eval days_open=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where last_status IN ("Draft","Investigating") AND days_open>30
| table register_id, incident_title, last_status, owner, days_open, last_update
| sort - days_open
```

Understanding this SPL

**Breach Log / Incident Register ‚Äî Immutable Chronological Record (¬ß164.402 / policy)** ‚Äî Maintains a searchable chronological register of all breach-suspected and confirmed incidents with status transitions for OCR-style examinations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_register` (`register_id`, `incident_title`, `status`, `severity`, `owner`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:breach_register. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:breach_register", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by register_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **days_open** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where last_status IN ("Draft","Investigating") AND days_open>30` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Breach Log / Incident Register ‚Äî Immutable Chronological Record (¬ß164.402 / policy)**): table register_id, incident_title, last_status, owner, days_open, last_update
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (status transitions), Table (stale investigations), Single value (open incidents >30d).
